attributes.action: Call for a General Election if a Prime Minister resigns
attributes.additional_details:
Under the current system, when a Prime Minister resigns, the governing party can choose a new leader who becomes Prime Minister without a national vote. This allows for significant changes in policy, direction, and cabinet composition without a direct mandate from the British electorate
​We believe that the British public should have a say in who leads the country and call on the Government to ensure that the power to choose a leader is returned to the voters whenever the incumbent leaves office
attributes.background: ​Update the Fixed-term Parliaments Act (or relevant constitutional legislation) to mandate that a General Election must be held within 60 days of a Prime Minister resigning their office.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition, which calls for similar action: "The removal or death of a PM should trigger a general election" https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/760199
